university-of-washington-bsc-biology.overview: UW Biology applicants first apply to the University and then follow Biology's major-entry requirements. The department's Fall 2026 degree update confirms a BS pathway with upper-division study across molecular/cellular/developmental biology, organismal physiology and ecology/evolution.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need UW undergraduate admission plus the Biology department's current major requirements; international applicants must also meet UW's English-proficiency policy.
items: International first-year applicants follow UW's first-year admission requirements.
International transfer applicants follow UW's transfer route and must review Biology major-entry rules.
Plan for Biology's prerequisite and capacity requirements before assuming major admission.
English proficiency must meet UW's current international undergraduate standard.

applicationSteps: Choose first-year or transfer status in the UW undergraduate application.
Submit the UW application and the required international academic and English evidence.
Review Biology's current admissions and major-requirements page for the intended entry year.
After UW admission, complete any required Biology major-entry process and advising steps.

academic: Secondary-school transcript or postsecondary transcripts for the selected UW application type.
Evidence of preparation in biology and related sciences where required by the major pathway.
Official English-proficiency score meeting UW's current policy.
application: Completed UW undergraduate application.
Required UW personal and academic-history responses.
Final official transcript if admitted and enrolling.

university-of-washington-bsc-biology.deadlinesNote: UW's published first-year autumn application deadline is November 15; transfer and major-entry dates can differ, so confirm the cycle-specific Biology and UW calendar.

university-of-washington-bsc-chemistry.overview: The UW BS in Chemistry is an advanced laboratory degree. Admission starts with UW undergraduate admission, followed by the Chemistry department's entry pathway; the degree itself requires calculus, physics, general and organic chemistry, laboratory work and a cumulative and science GPA of 2.5.

eligibility.intro: Applicants must satisfy UW undergraduate admission and Chemistry major-entry requirements, with English proficiency for international applicants.
items: International first-year applicants use UW's first-year application route.
International transfer applicants use UW's transfer route and should check Chemistry prerequisite equivalencies.
The published BS requirements include a 2.5 cumulative GPA and 2.5 science GPA.
Required chemistry courses must meet the department's stated minimum grade standard.

applicationSteps: Apply to UW through the correct first-year or transfer route.
Submit the academic records and English evidence required by UW Admissions.
Complete the Chemistry entrance pathway and prerequisite coursework described by the department.
Use the Chemistry checklist and advising process to confirm the degree plan after entry.

academic: Secondary-school or college transcripts for the UW application type.
Records showing preparation in chemistry, mathematics and physics.
Official English-proficiency score meeting UW's current policy.
application: Completed UW undergraduate application.
Required UW essays and academic-history information.
Final official transcripts after an offer, when requested by UW.

university-of-washington-bsc-chemistry.deadlinesNote: UW's published first-year autumn deadline is November 15. Chemistry major-entry timing and transfer deadlines are separate and must be checked for the intended cycle.

university-of-washington-bsc-mathematics.overview: The UW BS in Mathematics begins through UW undergraduate admission and continues through the Mathematics department's undergraduate pathway. The degree is built around calculus, linear algebra and advanced mathematical study, so applicants should map prior preparation before applying.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need UW undergraduate admission and must meet the Mathematics programme's current entry and degree requirements; international applicants also need UW English proficiency.
items: International first-year applicants follow UW's first-year requirements.
International transfer applicants follow UW's transfer requirements and should check course transferability.
Prior preparation in calculus and mathematics is important for the published degree sequence.
English proficiency must meet UW's current international undergraduate policy.

applicationSteps: Select first-year or transfer status and apply through UW Admissions.
Submit the required transcripts, academic information and English evidence.
Review Mathematics admissions and advising guidance for the intended entry year.
Complete placement, prerequisite and major-planning steps after admission as directed by the department.

academic: Secondary-school or postsecondary transcripts for the selected application route.
Evidence of mathematics preparation, especially calculus where applicable.
Official English-proficiency score meeting UW's policy.
application: Completed UW undergraduate application.
Required UW essays and academic-history responses.
Final official academic records if requested after admission.

university-of-washington-bsc-mathematics.deadlinesNote: UW's published first-year autumn deadline is November 15; transfer and department advising dates should be verified for the selected cycle.

university-of-washington-bsc-physics.overview: The UW BS in Physics combines calculus, introductory and upper-division physics, mathematical methods and laboratory work. Applicants first use UW undergraduate admission and should then verify the Physics department's current degree availability and entry requirements.

eligibility.intro: Applicants must meet UW undergraduate admission requirements and the Physics degree's current prerequisites; international applicants must meet UW English proficiency.
items: International first-year applicants apply through UW's first-year route.
International transfer applicants must confirm how prior physics and mathematics courses transfer.
Calculus and physics preparation are central to the published BS sequence.
Check whether the intended Physics option is accepting new students for the chosen cycle.

applicationSteps: Apply to UW as a first-year or transfer applicant.
Provide the academic records and English evidence required by UW Admissions.
Review Physics degree requirements and current availability before selecting the pathway.
Complete placement and advising steps for the calculus, physics and laboratory sequence after admission.

academic: Secondary-school or college transcripts for the application type.
Evidence of mathematics and physics preparation.
Official English-proficiency score meeting UW's current policy.
application: Completed UW undergraduate application.
Required UW personal and academic-history responses.
Final official transcript when requested by UW after admission.

university-of-washington-bsc-physics.deadlinesNote: UW's published first-year autumn deadline is November 15; Physics and transfer timing should be confirmed on the live department and UW calendars.

university-of-washington-bsc-nursing.overview: UW's BSN is a professional nursing pathway with prerequisites, nursing coursework, simulation and supervised clinical learning. Applicants must use the School of Nursing's current BSN admissions instructions in addition to meeting UW and international applicant requirements.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need UW admission plus the School of Nursing's published prerequisite and professional-programme requirements; clinical and licensing rules are separate from admission.
items: Complete the prerequisite coursework specified by the UW School of Nursing.
Meet UW undergraduate admission and international English-proficiency requirements.
Follow the BSN cohort and application rules published by the School of Nursing.
Understand that nursing registration depends on the regulator in the intended practice jurisdiction.

applicationSteps: Apply to UW under the application type specified by the School of Nursing.
Complete the BSN prerequisite coursework and confirm prerequisite equivalencies.
Submit the School of Nursing application and required materials by its current cycle deadline.
Complete any selection, advising, health or clinical-clearance steps required after an offer.

academic: Secondary-school or college transcripts showing BSN prerequisite study.
Evidence of completion of the nursing prerequisite courses.
Official English-proficiency evidence required for international applicants.
application: UW undergraduate application where required.
School of Nursing BSN application and required responses.
Any current programme forms or clearance documents listed by the School of Nursing.

university-of-washington-ms-computer-sci.overview: The Allen School MS in Computer Science and Engineering is a graduate programme requiring relevant preparation and programme-specific review. Applicants use the Allen School and UW Graduate School instructions, with research or advanced technical fit assessed by the programme.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or equivalent preparation in computer science or a closely related field and must meet the Allen School's current graduate requirements.
items: Hold a bachelor's degree or foreign equivalent by the required enrolment point.
Show preparation in core computer science and quantitative subjects expected by the Allen School.
Meet the programme's English-proficiency policy where applicable.
Review the current MS pathway, funding and cohort rules rather than assuming PhD requirements apply.

applicationSteps: Read the Allen School MS admissions page and current UW Graduate School instructions.
Create the required graduate application and select the Computer Science and Engineering MS.
Upload the programme's academic, statement, recommendation and test materials.
Submit by the live Allen School deadline and monitor requests for missing materials.

academic: Bachelor's transcript and degree evidence or foreign equivalent.
Academic record showing computer science, mathematics and related preparation.
English-proficiency score if required by the current Allen School policy.
application: Statement of purpose describing preparation and programme fit.
Letters of recommendation required by the current application checklist.
Resume or CV and any additional materials requested by the Allen School.

university-of-washington-ms-data-science.overview: UW's one-year Master of Data Science combines statistical modelling, data management, machine learning, visualisation, software engineering, ethics and a two-quarter capstone. The graduate listing is cycle-sensitive and must be checked for international eligibility before applying.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or foreign equivalent plus the programme's stated mathematics and computer-science preparation; international eligibility can vary by cycle.
items: Hold a bachelor's degree or recognised foreign equivalent.
Demonstrate the core mathematics, statistics, programming and data preparation expected by the programme.
Check the live graduate listing to confirm whether international or visa applicants are accepted for the intended autumn cycle.
Meet the programme's English-language requirement where applicable.

applicationSteps: Read the current MDS programme overview and graduate application listing.
Confirm the cycle's applicant-eligibility and visa-compatibility status.
Submit the UW graduate application with academic records and programme materials.
Complete any programme-specific review steps and respond to requests for supporting evidence.

academic: Bachelor's transcript and degree evidence or foreign equivalent.
Evidence of mathematics, statistics, programming and data-science preparation.
English-proficiency evidence when required by the current listing.
application: UW graduate application and programme-specific responses.
Resume or CV describing relevant technical or analytical work.
Any recommendations or supplemental materials listed in the live application portal.

university-of-washington-ms-business-ana.overview: UW Foster's MS in Business Analytics is a full-time, 12-month STEM-designated graduate programme. Admissions are handled through Foster and assess the applicant's quantitative, analytical and business preparation for an intensive industry-facing curriculum.

eligibility.intro: Applicants must satisfy Foster graduate admission requirements and show the quantitative, analytical and English preparation stated for the MSBA cohort.
items: Hold a bachelor's degree or equivalent before enrolment.
Demonstrate quantitative and analytical readiness for graduate business analytics study.
Meet Foster's English-language requirement or an approved exemption.
Confirm the current full-time, 12-month format and international eligibility before applying.

applicationSteps: Review Foster's MSBA admissions and cohort information.
Complete the Foster graduate application and pay any required application fee.
Submit transcripts, test evidence and the programme's written and recommendation materials.
Check the applicant portal until the file is complete and respond to any Foster request.

academic: Bachelor's transcript and degree certificate or equivalent.
Evidence of quantitative and analytical preparation.
English-proficiency score if required by Foster's current policy.
application: Resume or CV showing academic, professional and analytical experience.
Required essays or statements for the MSBA application.
Recommendations and any test scores or supplemental materials listed by Foster.

university-of-washington-ms-finance.overview: UW Foster's MS in Finance is a specialised one-year graduate finance programme. Applicants are reviewed through Foster's programme-specific process, with quantitative preparation and fit for advanced financial study central to the application.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or equivalent and the quantitative, English and programme preparation required by Foster for the current MS Finance cohort.
items: Hold a bachelor's degree or equivalent before enrolment.
Show quantitative preparation suitable for graduate finance coursework.
Meet Foster's English-language requirement or a published exemption.
Check the current cohort format, international eligibility and prerequisite list before applying.

applicationSteps: Read the live Foster MS Finance admissions and programme-cost information.
Complete Foster's graduate application for the MS Finance cohort.
Upload transcripts, quantitative/test evidence and the required written materials.
Submit recommendations and any remaining checklist items before the programme deadline.

academic: Bachelor's transcript and degree evidence or equivalent.
Evidence of mathematics, statistics or other quantitative preparation.
English-proficiency evidence if required by Foster.
application: Resume or CV.
Programme essays or statement of purpose required by Foster.
Recommendations and any test or supplemental materials listed in the live application.

university-of-washington-ms-information-.overview: UW Foster's MS in Information Systems is a 12-month fee-based graduate programme linking technology, data, systems and business decision-making. Applicants follow Foster's programme-specific application and prerequisite instructions rather than a generic UW route.

eligibility.intro: Applicants must meet Foster graduate requirements and show the academic or professional preparation requested for the MSIS cohort.
items: Hold a bachelor's degree or equivalent before the programme begins.
Meet the MSIS prerequisite and preparation requirements published by Foster.
Meet the programme's English-language requirement where applicable.
Review the fee-based, 12-month format and international enrolment conditions before applying.

applicationSteps: Review Foster's MSIS admissions, prerequisites and tuition pages.
Complete the Foster application for the MS in Information Systems.
Submit transcripts, resume, statements, recommendations and required test evidence.
Track the application portal and complete any follow-up items requested by Foster.

academic: Bachelor's transcript and degree evidence or equivalent.
Evidence of preparation relevant to information systems, technology or business.
English-proficiency score if required by the current MSIS policy.
application: Resume or CV detailing technology, systems or business experience.
MSIS essays or statement materials listed by Foster.
Recommendations and any required test or supplemental documents.

university-of-washington-ms-supply-chain.overview: UW Foster's Master of Supply Chain Management is a specialised graduate programme covering operations, sourcing, logistics, analytics and supply-chain strategy. Applicants must use the current Foster programme page for the exact cohort format, prerequisites and checklist.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or equivalent and must meet Foster's current graduate and supply-chain preparation requirements.
items: Hold a bachelor's degree or equivalent before enrolment.
Show preparation suited to operations, analytics, business or supply-chain study.
Meet Foster's English-language requirement or an approved exemption.
Confirm the current format and international eligibility for the selected cohort.

applicationSteps: Read Foster's supply-chain programme and graduate admissions information.
Complete the Foster graduate application for the Master of Supply Chain Management.
Submit academic records, resume, statements, recommendations and required test evidence.
Monitor the application checklist and answer any Foster follow-up request.

academic: Bachelor's transcript and degree evidence or equivalent.
Evidence of preparation in quantitative, business, operations or related subjects.
English-proficiency evidence if required by Foster.
application: Resume or CV showing relevant study or work experience.
Programme essays or statement of purpose.
Recommendations and any other materials listed in the current Foster checklist.

university-of-washington-mba.overview: The UW Foster Full-Time MBA is a two-year graduate business programme with a separate Foster admissions route. The school publishes round-based review and identifies a final round for international applicants, so candidates should use the live cycle calendar rather than a generic deadline.

eligibility.intro: Applicants must meet Foster's Full-Time MBA requirements and demonstrate readiness for the programme's integrated core, electives and career-focused study.
items: Hold a bachelor's degree or equivalent before enrolment.
Meet Foster's current MBA academic, English and test requirements or approved alternatives.
Provide the professional and leadership evidence requested by the MBA application.
International applicants must use the international applicant instructions and observe the published final round.

applicationSteps: Review Foster's Full-Time MBA admissions guide and round calendar.
Complete the Foster MBA application for the selected intake.
Submit transcripts, resume, essays, recommendations and required test or English evidence.
Complete any interview or post-submission steps requested by Foster.

academic: Bachelor's transcript and degree evidence or equivalent.
English-proficiency score or approved waiver where required.
GMAT/GRE or other accepted test evidence if required by the current MBA policy.
application: Professional resume or CV.
MBA essays and short-answer responses.
Recommendations and interview materials requested by Foster.

university-of-washington-mba.deadlinesNote: Foster identifies Round 3 as the final round for international applicants for the published cycle. Confirm the live date and earlier round options on Foster's MBA calendar.

university-of-washington-mph.overview: UW's Master of Public Health is a multidisciplinary graduate programme with a common core, concentration study and a required practicum. MPH applicants use SOPHAS and the UW Graduate School application, with concentration-specific requirements checked through the School of Public Health.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or equivalent and must satisfy the School of Public Health's MPH prerequisites, English policy and concentration requirements.
items: Hold a bachelor's degree or equivalent before enrolment.
Meet the prerequisite and concentration requirements listed for the selected MPH route.
Use the required SOPHAS and UW Graduate School application combination.
Meet English-proficiency requirements and be able to complete the required practicum.

applicationSteps: Select the UW MPH concentration and read its current admissions page.
Complete and submit the SOPHAS application with the required documents.
Complete the UW Graduate School application linked to the MPH route.
Monitor both application systems and complete any School of Public Health follow-up.

academic: Bachelor's transcript and degree evidence or equivalent.
Evidence of prerequisite preparation for the selected MPH concentration.
English-proficiency evidence where required for international applicants.
application: SOPHAS application and required personal statement.
Recommendations and resume or CV as listed for the selected concentration.
UW Graduate School application materials and any concentration supplement.

university-of-washington-mpa.overview: The Evans School Master of Public Administration is a graduate public-service degree combining policy, management, quantitative preparation and applied study. Applicants follow Evans and UW Graduate School instructions, with international candidates checking the current financial and English documentation requirements.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or foreign equivalent and the Evans School's current academic, quantitative and application preparation.
items: Hold a bachelor's degree or foreign equivalent before enrolment.
Meet Evans' quantitative and academic preparation expectations.
Satisfy the current English-language requirement for international applicants.
Review the MPA's applied curriculum and current international funding documentation instructions.

applicationSteps: Read the Evans MPA admissions page and UW Graduate School application instructions.
Select the MPA in the UW graduate application.
Upload transcripts, statement, resume, recommendations and required English evidence.
Submit by the live Evans deadline and monitor the graduate application portal.

academic: Bachelor's transcript and foreign-equivalent degree evidence.
Evidence of quantitative preparation required by Evans.
English-proficiency score if required by the current policy.
application: Statement of purpose addressing public-service interests and MPA fit.
Resume or CV.
Recommendations and any additional Evans application responses.

university-of-washington-mpp.overview: The Evans School Master of Public Policy focuses on policy analysis, economics, quantitative methods and applied public decision-making. Applicants use the Evans and UW Graduate School route and should verify the current cycle's prerequisite and funding instructions.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or foreign equivalent and must meet Evans' quantitative, academic and English requirements for the MPP.
items: Hold a bachelor's degree or foreign equivalent before enrolment.
Show the quantitative preparation expected for policy analysis and economics study.
Meet the current English-language requirement for international applicants.
Review the MPP's programme-specific deadlines, prerequisites and funding guidance.

applicationSteps: Read Evans' MPP admissions page and the UW Graduate School application guide.
Choose the MPP in the UW graduate application.
Submit transcripts, statement, resume, recommendations and English evidence where required.
Complete the live-cycle checklist and monitor the application portal for follow-up requests.

academic: Bachelor's transcript and foreign-equivalent degree evidence.
Evidence of mathematics, statistics or quantitative preparation.
English-proficiency score if required by Evans or UW.
application: MPP statement of purpose focused on policy interests and fit.
Resume or CV.
Recommendations and any programme-specific responses listed by Evans.

university-of-washington-med.overview: UW uses the Master of Education title across multiple College of Education pathways rather than one single curriculum. Applicants must choose the exact MEd route, then follow that pathway's admissions, practicum, certification and international-eligibility instructions.

eligibility.intro: Eligibility depends on the selected UW College of Education MEd pathway; applicants must verify its academic, English, practicum and certification rules.
items: Select a specific MEd pathway before relying on admission requirements.
Meet the chosen pathway's degree and prerequisite requirements.
Confirm whether the pathway accepts international applicants and the intended delivery format.
Check certification and practicum conditions separately because they vary by pathway and jurisdiction.

applicationSteps: Use the College of Education graduate programmes page to select the exact MEd pathway.
Read that pathway's current admissions page and application instructions.
Submit the UW graduate application with the route-specific documents and English evidence.
Complete any pathway interview, writing sample, portfolio or practicum checks listed by the programme.

academic: Bachelor's transcript and degree evidence or equivalent.
Pathway-specific prerequisite or professional-preparation records.
English-proficiency evidence where required by the selected MEd route.
application: Statement or personal essay required by the chosen pathway.
Resume or CV and recommendations when listed by the pathway.
Any portfolio, writing sample or certification materials specified by the programme.

university-of-washington-march.overview: UW's Master of Architecture is a professional graduate pathway in design, history, theory, representation and building technology. The programme length and prerequisite route depend on prior architectural education, so applicants must use the College of Built Environments' current MArch admissions page.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need the prior architectural preparation required for the selected MArch pathway and must meet UW Graduate School, portfolio and English requirements.
items: Hold a bachelor's degree or equivalent before enrolment.
Confirm the correct MArch track based on prior architectural study.
Submit the portfolio and prerequisite evidence required by the architecture programme.
Understand that professional licensure is governed by the jurisdiction where a graduate seeks registration.

applicationSteps: Select the MArch pathway and review its current prerequisite and portfolio instructions.
Complete the UW Graduate School application for the architecture programme.
Upload transcripts, portfolio, statement, recommendations and English evidence where required.
Monitor the application portal for portfolio or programme follow-up requests.

academic: Bachelor's transcript and degree evidence or equivalent.
Records of prior architecture, design, history or technical preparation as required by the track.
English-proficiency score if required for international applicants.
application: Architecture portfolio in the format specified by UW.
Statement of purpose and resume or CV.
Recommendations and any supplemental materials requested by the programme.

university-of-washington-master-sustaina.overview: UW's Master of Sustainability is an interdisciplinary graduate degree addressing environmental, social and organisational challenges. Applicants must verify the current programme format, prerequisites, fee status and graduate application route for the intended cycle.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or equivalent and the sustainability programme's current academic, English and pathway requirements.
items: Hold a bachelor's degree or equivalent before enrolment.
Show academic or professional preparation relevant to interdisciplinary sustainability study.
Meet the programme's English-language requirement where applicable.
Confirm the current format, campus, fee status and international eligibility before applying.

applicationSteps: Read the UW Environment Master of Sustainability admissions page.
Confirm the current programme route and required prerequisite preparation.
Submit the UW graduate application with transcripts, statement, resume and recommendations as listed.
Complete the application checklist and respond to programme requests before review.

academic: Bachelor's transcript and degree evidence or equivalent.
Evidence of preparation relevant to environmental, social or organisational sustainability.
English-proficiency evidence if required by the programme.
application: Statement of purpose addressing sustainability interests and programme fit.
Resume or CV.
Recommendations and any supplemental responses listed on the live application page.

university-of-washington-master-forestry.overview: UW SEFS offers graduate forestry routes built around forest ecosystems, natural resources, management, field learning and research. Applicants must select the exact Master of Forestry pathway and follow SEFS's current graduate admissions instructions.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need relevant academic preparation for the SEFS forestry pathway and must meet UW Graduate School and English requirements.
items: Hold a bachelor's degree or equivalent before enrolment.
Show preparation relevant to forestry, natural resources, ecology or a related field.
Meet the selected SEFS pathway's prerequisites and field-learning expectations.
International applicants must meet the current English and financial-documentation requirements.

applicationSteps: Review SEFS's current master's pathways and admissions page.
Select the Master of Forestry route and confirm prerequisites and field requirements.
Submit the UW graduate application with transcripts, statement, resume, recommendations and English evidence where required.
Monitor the graduate application portal and complete any SEFS follow-up.

academic: Bachelor's transcript and degree evidence or equivalent.
Evidence of forestry, ecology, natural-resources or related preparation.
English-proficiency score if required by UW or SEFS.
application: Statement of purpose describing forestry interests and fit.
Resume or CV with relevant field, research or professional experience.
Recommendations and any SEFS supplemental materials requested.

university-of-washington-jd.overview: The UW School of Law Juris Doctor is a three-year professional law degree with a separate law-school admissions route. Applicants must follow UW Law's current credential, LSAC, English and international-applicant instructions; a JD does not itself determine bar eligibility in another jurisdiction.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need a bachelor's degree or equivalent and must satisfy UW Law's JD admissions requirements, including the separate professional application process.
items: Hold a bachelor's degree or equivalent before JD enrolment.
Use the law-school application route and the credential process required by UW Law.
Meet current LSAC, English and academic requirements for international applicants.
Check bar and professional-licensing rules with the intended jurisdiction separately from admission.

applicationSteps: Read the UW School of Law JD admissions page and current international guidance.
Create the required LSAC/CAS materials and complete the UW Law application.
Submit transcripts, personal statement, resume, recommendations and test or English evidence as required.
Complete any interview or supplemental step requested by UW Law.

academic: Bachelor's transcript and degree evidence or equivalent.
Credential evaluation or international academic records required by the law-school checklist.
English-proficiency evidence if required by UW Law.
application: LSAC/CAS application materials.
JD personal statement and resume.
Recommendations and any optional or required essays listed by UW Law.

university-of-washington-phd-computer-sc.overview: The Allen School PhD in Computer Science and Engineering is a research doctorate evaluated for academic preparation, research fit and faculty-area alignment. Applicants use the Allen School and UW Graduate School route; the school states that admitted PhD students receive full financial support under the offer terms.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need strong relevant preparation and research fit for the Allen School's doctoral areas, plus UW's graduate and English requirements.
items: Hold a bachelor's or advanced degree with strong computer-science or related preparation.
Demonstrate research interests that fit the Allen School's doctoral areas and faculty.
Meet the current English-proficiency policy for international doctoral applicants.
Review the programme's funding, application and deadline rules for the intended cycle.

applicationSteps: Review the Allen School PhD areas, faculty and current admissions instructions.
Complete the UW Graduate School application for Computer Science and Engineering PhD study.
Submit transcripts, statement, research interests, recommendations and required English evidence.
Complete the research-fit and funding review process described by the Allen School.

academic: Transcripts and degree evidence for all relevant university study.
Evidence of advanced computer-science, mathematics or related preparation.
English-proficiency score if required by the current doctoral policy.
application: Research-focused statement of purpose.
Resume or CV including research, publications or technical work where applicable.
Letters of recommendation and any supplemental materials requested by the Allen School.

university-of-washington-phd-business.overview: The Foster School PhD in Business Administration is a research doctorate organised around discipline-specific scholarship, methods and faculty research fit. Applicants must identify the relevant doctoral area and use Foster's current doctoral application and funding guidance.

eligibility.intro: Applicants need strong academic preparation and research fit for a Foster doctoral area, with English and discipline-specific requirements confirmed for the current cycle.
items: Hold a bachelor's or advanced degree with preparation relevant to the selected business discipline.
Show quantitative, methodological or discipline preparation required by the chosen doctoral area.
Demonstrate research fit with the relevant Foster faculty and field.
Meet Foster and UW English requirements and review current funding terms.

applicationSteps: Choose the Foster doctoral area and review its current admissions page.
Complete the UW Graduate School/Foster doctoral application for that area.
Submit transcripts, research statement, resume, recommendations and required test or English evidence.
Check the portal for department follow-up, interview or funding information.

academic: Transcripts and degree evidence for prior university study.
Evidence of quantitative, methods or discipline preparation relevant to the selected area.
English-proficiency score if required by Foster or UW.
application: Research statement describing interests, questions and Foster fit.
Resume or CV including research or professional experience.
Recommendations and any area-specific writing sample or test materials requested by Foster.
